name: Nomic Contrastors
tagline: Train and evaluate contrastive text and multimodal embedding models
metaDescription: Contrastors is Nomic's open contrastive learning toolkit for training and evaluating text and multimodal embedding models like nomic-embed.
overview: Contrastors is Nomic AI's contrastive learning toolkit. It holds the training code and recipes behind the fully open nomic-embed family of long-context text and multimodal embedding models, so researchers and engineers can train and evaluate contrastive models themselves.

It is aimed at teams who want to build their own embedding models rather than only consume a hosted API. It is built on Flash Attention, supports multi-GPU training, and uses GradCache so you can train with large batch sizes even when GPU memory is limited.

Within the embedding-models category, it sits on the training side: it covers masked language modeling pretraining, contrastive pretraining and finetuning, Matryoshka representation learning for flexible embedding sizes, and CLIP/LiT style training for vision.
features: Built on Flash Attention for fast, memory-efficient training
Multi-GPU training with DeepSpeed and torchrun
GradCache support for large batch sizes in limited GPU memory
Matryoshka Representation Learning for flexible embedding sizes
CLIP and LiT style contrastive learning for vision, plus ViT/timm model loading
Hugging Face support for common models (Pythia/GPTNeoX, BERT) and MLM pretraining

gettingStarted.intro: Contrastors relies on custom CUDA kernels from Flash Attention, so set up a Python environment with CUDA 11.8+ before installing. Verify your CUDA version with nvcc --version.

steps.heading: Create and activate a virtual environment
steps.body: Use a clean Python venv to keep the CUDA and Flash Attention dependencies isolated.

steps.heading: Install torch and build tools
steps.body: Install torch (see the PyTorch docs for the right command for your system), then the build helpers Flash Attention needs.
steps.code:
pip3 install torch torchvision torchaudio
pip install wheel packaging ninja setuptools

steps.heading: Install Flash Attention kernels and the package
steps.body: Install Flash Attention with its custom kernels, then install contrastors itself in editable mode.

steps.heading: Run contrastive pretraining
steps.body: Launch a training run with torchrun across your GPUs, pointing at one of the provided configs.

useCases: Train your own BERT-based text embedding model from scratch with MLM pretraining followed by contrastive pretraining and finetuning
Reproduce or adapt the nomic-embed-text-v1 training recipe on your own corpus
Fit large effective batch sizes on memory-limited GPUs using GradCache
Train CLIP/LiT style multimodal embeddings and produce Matryoshka embeddings with flexible output sizes
